response: Japan, a nation of 125 million people, maintains a GDP of $4.2 trillion with a growth rate of 1.1% and generates $756 billion in exports. Despite a low unemployment rate of 2.6%, the country faces significant economic headwinds, including inflation at 3.2% and a towering debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%. The population also enjoys one of the longest life expectancies globally, averaging 84.6 years.
